name: hecateflow
description: Reusable, MCU-agnostic embedded-development skill system (13 skills) distilled from a real multi-core bare-metal C project. v1.2 adds autonomy-first multi-model orchestration: self-verification first, proactive read-only subagents, review chains, gated write workers, and user-confirmed Git.

interface.shortDescription: Embedded-dev lifecycle skills with autonomous discovery, proactive read-only subagents, gated workers, and Git confirmation.
interface.longDescription: HecateFlow orchestrates the full embedded-development lifecycle with 13 interactive, MCU-agnostic skills: workspace and per-target initialization, incremental module design, incremental implementation, behavior-preserving refactor, deep review, and an always-on per-edit safety gate (volatile/ISR/numeric/peripheral). v1.2 treats multi-model collaboration as autonomy-first authority partitioning, not extra writers: the main agent verifies discoverable facts before asking, keeps context, code ownership, and Git authority; read-only subagents are proactively delegated by complexity to explore, plan, review, and challenge evidence; high-risk conclusions pass through a review chain and main-agent verification; write-capable workers are delayed until the user has explicitly asked to implement/modify/land/apply a patch (用户已明确要求实现/修改/落地/应用补丁), the implementation plan is determined, file scopes are disjoint, and worker boundaries are explicit. A persistent project manifest (.hecateflow/project.json) remembers MCU family, toolchain, build system, targets, scenario constraints, owned peripherals, interaction defaults, and Git confirmation gates; minimal questions are limited to external unverifiable facts, safety boundaries, hardware/physical risk, write-worker scope escalation risk, and Git/remote history changes, and stage/commit/push require current-change-set confirmation after summary/tests/suggested commit message/files-to-stage.
defaultPrompt: 初始化这个嵌入式工作区
